channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Core inflation rate hit 3.4% in May, highest since October 2023, Fed’s preferred gauge shows
item.description: The personal consumption expenditures price index was expected to show a 4.1% annual increase.

item.title: Bayer wins Supreme Court case making it harder to sue over glyphosate in Monsanto's Roundup
item.description: Glyphosate, used in Roundup weedkiller, is the most commonly used weedkiller in agriculture, and it has long been linked to cancer claims.

item.title: Apple stock drops 5% on MacBook and iPad price hikes due to memory crunch
item.description: Tim Cook said last week that Apple would hike prices due to surging memory and storage costs.

item.title: GM reveals 2027 GMC Sierra pickup with new V-8 engines, redesigned styling
item.description: The new GMC Sierra trucks are crucial to GM's sales and earnings, especially highly profitable luxury Denali and off-road AT4 models.

item.title: Micron is up 10% after blockbuster earnings, but has pulled back from highs
item.description: The company's revenue more than quadrupled from $9.3 billion a year earlier to $41.46 billion, it reported on Wednesday

item.title: Josh Brown says this bank stock on his list has a 'holy trinity' setup going for it
item.description: Josh Brown and Sean Russo go deeper on this banking name on their Best Stocks in the Market list.

item.title: JPMorgan names Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh co-presidents as longtime exec Marianne Lake exits
item.description: The moves would appear to make Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh the most obvious possible successors to JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon.

item.title: Wendy's turns lower as meme rally fails to extend to a second day
item.description: The stock's volatile run appeared largely disconnected from company fundamentals and instead reflected a burst of social media enthusiasm.

item.title: Supreme Court lets Trump end deportation protections for Syrians and Haitians
item.description: TPS allows migrants from countries stricken by war, natural disaster or other catastrophes to live and work in the U.S. while it's unsafe to return to their home countries.

item.title: Chevron says no quick fix for gas prices as Trump takes on Big Oil: ‘It’s going to take time’
item.description: Her comments come shortly after President Donald Trump ordered an investigation into Big Oil, accusing them of "gouging" consumers.

item.title: TikTok and YouTube are reinventing sports viewership. Broadcasters are taking note
item.description: Leagues, teams and media networks are increasingly meeting younger fans on social media as platforms like TikTok, Roblox and YouTube capture most of their time.

item.title: Luxury spending now driven by experiences and 'inheritourism'
item.description: While luxury goods sales are expected to grow between 1% and 4%, experiences are on track for growth of between 3% and 7% this year, according to a new report.

item.title: GameStop’s CEO just sacrificed a $35 billion pay package. Here’s how it could impact his effort to buy eBay
item.description: Ryan Cohen has shared few details on how he'll move forward with his $56 billion offer for eBay since the company rejected the proposal in May.

item.title: Micron is soaring after blowout earnings report. That's good news for this other memory chipmaker, Citigroup says
item.description: Sandisk will likely benefit from the surge in memory demand that was behind Micron's extraordinary results reported Wednesday, according to Citi.

item.title: The world’s top-performing stock market still isn’t a developed market. Here’s why, according to the MSCI chief
item.description: MSCI CEO Henry Fernandez said South Korea's stock market still falls short of developed market standards.

item.title: Trump pledges rapid U.S. response for Venezuela after historic earthquakes kill hundreds
item.description: The swift U.S. offer of assistance reflects a degree of diplomatic realignment between the Trump administration and the Venezuelan interim government.

item.title: Iraq piles pressure on OPEC over quota dispute after UAE exit
item.description: Reuters reported that though Iraq had considered leaving OPEC, the ​current plan is to remain a member of the group while seeking a higher output quota.

item.title: Darden Restaurants earnings beat estimates but Olive Garden growth weakens
item.description: Same-store sales growth at Darden's fine-dining restaurants and Olive Garden fell short of expectations.

item.title: U.S. giving topped $600 billion for the first time last year. Megadonors and bequests are to thank
item.description: The stock market rally turbocharged charitable giving in 2025, but primarily from wealthy donors.

item.title: Strategy should halt bitcoin buys and bolster cash reserves, CryptoQuant says
item.description: Michael Saylor's Strategy should pause its bitcoin purchases and rebuild its cash reserves, according to a CryptoQuant report.

item.title: We're locking in triple-digit profits on an AI winner at record highs
item.description: The stock has soared this week for unclear reasons.

item.title: How much does debt settlement cost?
item.description: A debt settlement company could substantially lower your credit card bills. Find out what (and how) they charge for their services.

item.title: Iran warns ships it's 'unacceptable and dangerous' to transit the Strait of Hormuz without their approval
item.description: The stern warning underscores Tehran's resolve to retain control over the Strait of Hormuz and to resist transits that bypass its authorization.

item.title: Annuity options are growing in 401(k)s, but adoption remains limited
item.description: Annuity options are gaining ground in 401(k)s, amid worker concerns about retirement income and longevity risks.

item.title: Bitcoin is having a tough year. Traders are betting it's going to get worse
item.description: Options traders are treating this break like it could be the tip of an iceberg.

item.title: Anthropic's latest hiring spree reveals where it's building AI data centers next
item.description: The AI lab is hiring for AI data center roles in Australia and Japan as it rushes to expand compute capacity overseas.

item.title: Tesla to ramp up production in Germany by 20%
item.description: Tesla said on Thursday that production at its Berlin plant will rise by 20% to 7,500 vehicles per week from October this year.

item.title: Gold hovers around $4,000, silver holds below $60 — has the shimmer worn off the precious metal rally?
item.description: Gold is down almost 8% so far this year, while silver has shed more than 20%.

item.title: Germany's Merck boosts life sciences business with $11 billion Bio-Techne deal
item.description: German drugmaker Merck ​KGaA said on Thursday it will acquire U.S. biotech firm Bio-Techne for $11.3 billion, its largest deal in over a decade.

item.title: Micron is tech's new margin king as memory crisis pushes company past Nvidia and Meta
item.description: Micron said in its earnings report that its gross margin jumped to 84.9% from 39% a year ago.
